briefing.opening: Hello everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for June 23, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Global Climate Change Threats and Urgent Warnings
sections.content: Climate-related news released today clearly demonstrates that climate change is no longer a future threat but an ongoing, urgent disaster. Across Europe, record-breaking heatwaves have caused multiple fatalities in France and prompted red weather warnings in the UK as temperatures approached 40 degrees Celsius, reporting severe casualties and social disruption [132, 133, 147, 148, 149]. Domestically, a study found that the risk of death increases by 11% when heatwaves and ozone overlap, with the elderly and men being particularly vulnerable [31]. News that climate change-induced species extinction has occurred more frequently in temperate regions over the past 25 years warns of an ecological crisis [5]. The UN Secretary-General emphasized that climate change is no longer a distant threat [138]. Alongside criticism that global silence on climate change is unacceptable [130], Australians are filing human rights lawsuits over climate change damages, indicating a heightened sense of crisis in the international community [144].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Proactive Responses in Climate Change Adaptation and Agriculture
sections.content: Amidst these threats, diverse efforts to enhance climate change adaptability are being proactively pursued domestically. Chungnam Province has implemented measures and established mid-to-long-term plans to enhance its citizens' climate change adaptability [1, 30, 80], and a forum to explore response strategies for climate change and agricultural disasters is scheduled to be held in Jeju [3]. Particularly in the agricultural sector, the National Institute of Agricultural Sciences and aT have signed an MOU for climate change response collaboration, strengthening cooperation in agricultural product supply stabilization and long-term storage technologies [2, 22, 29]. Daegu Gunwi County is gaining attention for its climate-resilient agricultural technologies through the expansion of demonstration cultivation of summer leafy vegetables and the development of safe seedling cultivation techniques [10, 15, 21]. Furthermore, Gyeongnam Province is strengthening its response to complex disasters with the completion of multi-purpose check dams, and Jeju Province is considering the introduction of groundwater storage dams to address water scarcity amidst the climate crisis, demonstrating ongoing adaptation efforts tailored to regional characteristics [20, 19, 129]. International cooperation is also active, with public institutions, universities, and international organizations gathering to strengthen water management and climate change response capabilities in developing countries [23, 24, 141].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating the Transition to a Carbon-Neutral Society
sections.content: Various efforts to transition to a carbon-neutral society, a core aspect of climate change response, are accelerating both domestically and internationally. The Seoul Metropolitan Government and Korea Broadcasting News are planning to host a Climate Tech Conference seeking solutions to the climate crisis through AI technology [11, 36]. The Korean Society of Soil and Environmental Engineers is holding a climate response seminar at a CCU (Carbon Capture, Utilization) facility, indicating active movement towards adopting innovative technologies [32]. Coway is accelerating its carbon neutrality efforts by expanding its solar power plant at the Yugu factory, aiming to reduce annual greenhouse gas emissions by 320 tons [51, 53, 65, 67, 68, 82, 88, 105, 107, 118, 120, 124, 127]. Kumho Petrochemical is strengthening its ESG management by publishing a sustainability report that unveils its carbon neutrality roadmap and CCUS establishment plans [41, 49, 70, 74]. Furthermore, the Korea Environment Corporation is recruiting trainees for greenhouse gas specialists, focusing on nurturing practical carbon-neutral talent [37, 38, 45, 48, 61, 77]. Anseong City is encouraging citizens' participation in carbon neutrality practices through its Climate Action Opportunity Income platform [62], thereby promoting society-wide involvement. International efforts are also noteworthy, with Vietnamese companies joining hands to build a national brand of 'carbon neutrality' [84].

sections.title: 🌱 Efforts and Challenges for Renewable Energy Expansion
sections.content: The expansion of renewable energy is essential for achieving carbon neutrality goals, and progress and challenges in this sector are also drawing attention. Private investment is active, with the renewable energy investment platform 'Mohet' surpassing 500 billion Korean Won in cumulative investments and recording a 67% growth rate in just over a year [89, 94, 98, 103, 104, 108, 109, 114]. GS E&C is expanding its business with the goal of developing 3 trillion Korean Won worth of new and renewable energy projects [112, 119]. Meanwhile, regarding claims that renewable energy expansion causes large-scale blackouts, OECD and domestic fact-check reports have debunked these misconceptions, stating they are 'mostly false' and attributing the cause to grid failures [86, 90, 91, 93]. However, the wind and solar power industries are demanding revisions to current electricity market and grid operation rules, arguing that they hinder renewable energy expansion [101, 102, 117], making policy improvement an urgent task. The Korea Energy Agency is actively engaged in international cooperation, participating in 'Intersolar Europe 2026' in Germany to promote K-renewable energy technologies and support companies' entry into overseas markets [85, 92, 95, 96, 106].

briefing.closing: Through today's briefing, we have once again confirmed that the threat of climate change is becoming a reality, while also seeing that diverse domestic and international efforts to combat it are bearing fruit. Climate change adaptation, the transition to carbon neutrality, and the expansion of renewable energy are no longer options but necessities, requiring continued attention and effort going forward. This concludes the climate briefing for June 23, 2026.

To address the water scarcity crisis caused by climate change, the introduction of a 'groundwater storage dam' is being considered for the first time in Jeju.
